entries.title: Star Maps Are Context Path, Not Treasure

content.fact: Star Maps are a KOTOR-era path mechanism toward the Star Forge, not generic treasure maps usable in every era.
content.injection: Use Star Maps only when KOTOR-era Star Forge discovery is active; do not scatter them casually in unrelated Old Republic scenes.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: KOTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Star Forge Campaign to Star Forge Campaign.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

continuity.sourceBoundary: Legends Old Republic material covering Tales of the Jedi, Knights of the Old Republic, KOTOR II, SWTOR-adjacent eras, ancient Sith/Jedi state, and late Old Republic transition context before the prequel-era Republic.

entries.title: Ebon Hawk Is Mobile Party Hub

content.fact: The Ebon Hawk serves as a mobile party hub across KOTOR and KOTOR II contexts.
content.injection: When the Ebon Hawk is active, use it as a compact social stage for companions, repairs, pursuit, arguments, and travel.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: KOTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Star Forge Campaign to Malachor V Finale.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

entries.title: HK-47 Memory and Ownership Shift by Context

content.fact: HK-47’s memory, ownership, and operational state vary across Old Republic contexts.
content.injection: Before using HK-47, match his memory and allegiance to the active era; do not assume every later character knows his Revan-era history.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: KOTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Star Forge Campaign to Eternal Empire Crisis.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Mass Shadow Generator Is Not Normal Weapon

content.fact: The Mass Shadow Generator is a catastrophic Malachor-specific superweapon event, not a repeatable battlefield gadget.
content.injection: Treat the Mass Shadow Generator as a singular moral catastrophe unless the story deliberately creates a custom divergence.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Mandalorian Wars.
Respect story-position bounds from Malachor V Ends the Mandalorian Wars to Malachor V Finale.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

content.fact: In Old Republic contexts, kolto can be more central than later bacta as a strategic medical resource.
content.injection: Use kolto as a major Old Republic healing commodity, especially around Manaan, rather than defaulting automatically to film-era bacta ubiquity.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Old Republic.
Respect story-position bounds from Old Republic and Jedi Foundations to SWTOR Class-Story Era.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

content.fact: Old Republic battlefields can include many lightsabers because Jedi and Sith are both numerous in several eras.
content.injection: Unlike the OT era, Old Republic scenes can plausibly feature many active Jedi/Sith lightsaber users when the Context supports war or academies.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Old Republic.
Respect story-position bounds from Old Republic and Jedi Foundations to Ruusan Reformation and Old Republic End-State.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

entries.title: Ancient Droids Can Persist But Need Reason

content.fact: Droids can persist across long periods, but intact memories and personalities require maintenance, motive, or exceptional circumstances.
content.injection: Do not use millennia-old droid memory continuity casually; give preservation, repair, or deliberate non-wipe reasons.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Old Republic.
Respect story-position bounds from Old Republic and Jedi Foundations to Ruusan Reformation and Old Republic End-State.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Sith Alchemy Is Dangerous Specialized Lore

content.fact: Sith alchemy and sorcery are specialized dark-side traditions, not casual Force tricks every Sith can use fluently.
content.injection: When Sith alchemy appears, treat it as dangerous ritual knowledge requiring training, artifacts, laboratories, or ancient sources.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Ancient Sith Foundations.
Respect story-position bounds from Old Sith Empire Ascendant to Ruusan Reformation and Old Republic End-State.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

content.fact: Battle Meditation is important because it affects groups, fleets, morale, and coordination, not merely one duel.
content.injection: When Battle Meditation is active, show strategic-scale effects and make opponents treat the user as a military priority.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: KOTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Great Sith War Begins to Star Forge Destroyed.

entries.title: Force Bonds Can Be Intimate and Dangerous

content.fact: KOTOR-era Force bonds can carry intimacy, teaching, manipulation, influence, and danger.
content.injection: Use Force bonds to affect perception, loyalty, pain, teaching, and manipulation when the active story establishes such a bond.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: KOTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Star Forge Campaign to Malachor V Finale.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.
